mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate from its recent low signals a definitive turn in the business cycle, leading to reduced consumer spending, lower corporate earnings, and negative equity returns as recession probabilities are repriced.
evidence_refs: fact:The Sahm Rule, though formalized later, intuits that a rising unemployment rate is a strong real-time recession indicator.
failure_condition: In the 12 months following a trigger event, cumulative S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained year-over-year decline in housing starts reflects a deep contraction in residential investment, a key cyclical component of GDP. This weakness spills over into related sectors, depressing aggregate demand and leading to lower equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:The US housing market peaked in 2006 and has been in a steep decline, a major concern for the economy in late 2007.
failure_condition: The rolling 36-month correlation between the year-over-year change in HOUST and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Aggressive monetary easing, defined by a rapid series of cuts to the federal funds rate, lowers the discount rate for future earnings and signals central bank support for the economy and financial markets, boosting equity prices over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is cut by > 75bps over a 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve began cutting rates in September 2007 in response to growing financial stress.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward S&P 500 return in the month following a trigger event is not statistically higher than the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating momentum in industrial production is a leading indicator of a slowdown in the goods-producing sector of the economy. This points to lower capacity utilization and weaker corporate earnings, resulting in negative pressure on stock prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a core component of many leading economic indexes.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in INDPRO fails to exhibit a statistically significant positive rank correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while unemployment is also rising, it signals a stagflationary environment. This scenario severely constrains the Federal Reserve's ability to support the economy, leading to higher risk premia and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL change > 3% AND year-over-year UNRATE change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was a famously difficult period for equities.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of months where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real retail sales indicates that consumer spending, the largest component of the US economy, is faltering. This directly implies lower future revenues and earnings for consumer-facing companies, negatively impacting the broader market.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year change in real retail sales (RSAFS / CPIAUCSL) is negative
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approximately 70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the real retail sales growth signal for 20-day forward returns is not statistically significant and negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The monthly change in nonfarm payrolls is a timely and comprehensive measure of economic health. Positive surprises in payrolls growth lead to upward revisions of economic forecasts and boost investor sentiment, driving positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The monthly Employment Situation Report is one of the most closely watched economic releases.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 1-month change in PAYEMS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns (centered on the release date) is not positive and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is tightening monetary policy (rising FEDFUNDS) into an already-weakening housing market (falling HOUST), the combined effect severely restricts credit and crushes residential investment, accelerating an economic downturn and causing sharply negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in FEDFUNDS is positive AND 12-month change in HOUST is negative
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed was hiking rates from 2004-2006, which contributed to the bursting of the housing bubble.
failure_condition: During periods when the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically lower than the unconditional average.
